title: Solution Overview — The AquaVision Platform
the_platform_in_one_paragraph: AquaVision is an AI-powered aquaculture management platform built to serve fish farmers at every stage of the journey, not just the ones who can already afford sensors. Phase 1 is a free-to-low-cost app — an AI farming guide, a farm setup assistant, water quality logging, feeding schedules, photo-based disease identification, a farmer community, learning modules, and record keeping — the on-ramp that brings thousands of beginner and smallholder farmers onto the platform before they can pay for anything. Phase 2 turns that base into a business: a premium subscription layer adds analytics, predictive health monitoring, smart recommendations, a marketplace for feed and equipment and buyers, and paid expert consultations, monetising the farms that have outgrown a notebook. Phase 3 is where the platform grows a body: IoT sensors, in-situ water probes, and the OmniDrone/OmniDock system feed a digital twin (PondTwin) and an enterprise dashboard, giving commercial and enterprise operators automated monitoring and response at a scale no manual round can reach. These are not three products. They are one platform's growth path — from a record-keeping app to an autonomous sensing system — and each phase is only as credible as the data it has honestly earned so far.

why_phased_rather_than_hardware_first.statement: The old pitch was 'we build a drone.' The new one is 'we build the aquaculture management platform; the drone and IoT sensors are how the platform sees and acts for farms that have outgrown manual logging.'
why_phased_rather_than_hardware_first.reasoning: A hardware-first company has to defer smallholders on affordability grounds, because a drone-and-dock deployment only pencils out at commercial scale. A phased platform does not have that problem: smallholders are the acquisition engine in Phase 1, not a segment deferred until later. Revenue and engineering rigor still ramp with farm size and willingness to pay — they simply ramp inside one continuous product instead of gatekeeping the whole company behind hardware that has not flown.

product_line_at_a_glance.name: AquaVision App
product_line_at_a_glance.description: Free/low-cost mobile-and-web app: AI farming guide, farm setup assistant, manual/photo water quality logging, feeding schedules, photo-based disease ID, community, learning modules, record keeping.

product_line_at_a_glance.name: AquaVision Premium
product_line_at_a_glance.description: Subscription add-on to the App: analytics, predictive health monitoring, smart recommendations, marketplace (feed/equipment/buyers), paid expert consultations.

product_line_at_a_glance.name: AquaVision Platform
product_line_at_a_glance.phase: spans all three phases; is the Phase 3 backend specifically
product_line_at_a_glance.description: The cloud and edge software underneath all three phases: in Phase 1-2 it is the app/premium backend; in Phase 3 it additionally ingests, fuses, and models hardware telemetry, drives the digital twin, and serves the enterprise dashboard.

product_line_at_a_glance.name: OmniDrone
product_line_at_a_glance.description: The aerial platform: probe, sensing, dispensing, telemetry. A Phase 3 hardware upgrade, not the core product.

product_line_at_a_glance.name: OmniDock
product_line_at_a_glance.description: Shore-side automated dock: shelter, charge, probe rinse and decontamination, calibration reference, connectivity backhaul.

product_line_at_a_glance.name: PondTwin
product_line_at_a_glance.description: The digital twin surface presented to the farmer: a live, navigable model of the water body and its stock, built from Phase 3 sensor and drone data.

maturity_by_phase.name: Foundation — AquaVision App
maturity_by_phase.maturity: Pre-launch. No users, no traction data. Every feature listed is a design target until real farms are using it.

maturity_by_phase.name: Monetization — AquaVision Premium
maturity_by_phase.maturity: Concept. Depends on Phase 1 having accumulated enough real usage and logging data to make analytics and predictive features credible — a cold-start dependency, not a solved problem.

maturity_by_phase.name: Smart Aquaculture — IoT / OmniDrone / PondTwin
maturity_by_phase.maturity: TRL 3. The physics is established in the literature; the integration into this vehicle and this platform is not demonstrated in hardware. Nothing has flown.

phases.name: Foundation — the AquaVision App
phases.tagline: Free or low-cost. The acquisition and data-accumulation engine.
phases.audience: Beginners and smallholders establishing their first ponds — the segment a hardware-first drone pitch had to defer on affordability grounds. Here they are the top of the funnel, not a deferred segment.
phases.purpose: Attract thousands of beginner and smallholder farmers because the product is free or inexpensive, and start accumulating the farm-setup, water-quality, and feeding data that Phase 2's analytics and Phase 3's models will eventually need.
what_it_includes: Water quality logging — manual and photo entry; no sensors yet
phases.why_this_is_the_foundation: This is the user-acquisition and data-accumulation engine for the whole platform. Every later phase's credibility depends on this phase actually being used by real farmers, not on it merely existing.
phases.honest_note: Phase 1 has not launched. There are no users, no engagement data, and no evidence that a free tier converts to Phase 2 at any particular rate. The 'AI' in the farming guide and disease ID is a design target trained toward public and literature data until real farmer photos and logs exist to train on. `traction.json` stays at honest zero until that changes.

phases.name: Monetization — AquaVision Premium
phases.tagline: Subscription plus take-rate. Where AquaVision becomes a real business.
phases.audience: Farms that have grown past what manual tracking in the free app can support, and are willing to pay for an edge.
phases.purpose: Convert the free base into revenue via a premium layer built on the data Phase 1 has been quietly accumulating.
what_it_includes: Premium analytics on the farmer's own logged history
Predictive health monitoring — ML trained on Phase 1 data
Expert consultations — paid access to aquaculturists and vets
phases.why_this_is_the_business: This is where AquaVision becomes a subscription-plus-take-rate business on top of the free base, rather than a free tool with no revenue model.
phases.honest_note: Predictive health monitoring is only as good as the Phase 1 data it trains on, and Phase 1 has not launched — so this phase inherits a cold-start problem it cannot solve on its own timeline. The marketplace has the ordinary two-sided bootstrap problem: it needs sellers before it has value to buyers and buyers before sellers will list. Treat 'predictive' as `modeled_projection` language, not a measured capability, until there is a trained model and a held-out test set to point to.

phases.name: Smart Aquaculture — IoT, OmniDrone, and the Enterprise Layer
phases.tagline: Hardware as an upgrade layer. Sold to commercial and enterprise farms, not the whole company's core product.
phases.audience: Commercial and enterprise operators whose scale justifies automated sensing and who need monitoring and response beyond what manual logging, or even Phase 2 analytics on manually logged data, can deliver.
phases.purpose: Extend the platform with IoT sensors, in-situ water probes, autonomous aerial monitoring, a digital twin, and an enterprise dashboard — sold as a premium add-on to farms that have outgrown Phase 1 and Phase 2.
what_it_includes: IoT sensors and in-situ water probes
phases.why_this_is_not_the_core_product: The company is no longer 'we build a drone.' The drone and IoT sensors are how the platform sees and acts for the subset of farms that have outgrown manual logging — a Phase 3 upgrade, sold as hardware-as-upgrade / premium add-on, not the reason the company exists.

the_omnidrone_system.note: Everything below this point is the deep engineering content for the Phase 3 hardware layer specifically. It is unchanged from the original hardware-first pitch because it was already rigorous and honest — it is simply now scoped to Phase 3 rather than presented as the whole product.
the_omnidrone_system.the_system_in_one_paragraph: The OmniDrone is an autonomous multirotor that launches from a shore-side dock, flies a planned route across a fish farm, and at each station lowers a multiparameter sonde on a powered winch to profile the water column from surface to bottom. While it hovers, a calibrated multispectral and thermal payload images the surrounding water, and the probe's in-situ chemistry calibrates that imagery into real concentrations rather than relative indices. A carriage and ejection system dispenses feed, lime, conditioners, or treatments to specific coordinates. Everything streams to the AquaVision platform, which fuses point profiles with area imagery, drives a digital twin of the water body, and surfaces the result as an answer rather than a dataset.

the_four_functions.label: Profile — the reeled probe
the_four_functions.what_it_does: Lowers a sonde to measure dissolved oxygen, temperature, pH, ammonia, ORP, turbidity, chlorophyll-a, and conductivity at multiple depths at each station.
the_four_functions.why_it_is_the_core: Surface measurements cannot see stratification, and stratification is the precondition for the catastrophic failure mode. Every other function on this vehicle could be replicated by a competitor with an off-the-shelf drone. This one could not. If the winch fails, the product is a camera.

the_four_functions.label: Scan — surface anomaly detection
the_four_functions.what_it_does: Images the water surface in RGB, multispectral, and thermal, detecting floating mortalities, fish piping at the surface, oil sheen, foam, algal mats, debris, and net damage above the waterline.
the_four_functions.boundary: Sees the surface. Sunk mortalities and submerged structure are invisible to it, which caps the mortality-detection claim in species whose dead fish sink.

the_four_functions.label: Dispense — carriage and ejection
the_four_functions.what_it_does: Carries and meters granular feed, lime, and liquid conditioners or treatments to GPS-referenced coordinates.
the_four_functions.why_it_matters_more_than_it_looks: This is the answer to the strongest objection against the whole venture — that information without response capacity is just a countdown clock. A platform that can act on its own finding is a different proposition from one that can only report.

the_four_functions.label: Transmit — the data multiplex
the_four_functions.what_it_does: Streams telemetry, profiles, and imagery to the platform for fusion, AI/ML analysis, digital twin update, and dashboard presentation.

the_architectural_insight.statement: The probe and the camera are not two features. They are one instrument.
the_architectural_insight.explanation: A multispectral camera over inland water produces indices — NDCI, NDWI — which are relative numbers, not concentrations. Turning an index into a chlorophyll concentration requires a local regression against real measurements, and that regression is site-specific and drifts with conditions. Almost every UAV water quality study in the literature handles this by sending a boat out with a sonde. That is why almost none of them became products: the boat is the whole cost.
the_architectural_insight.what_we_do_differently: The vehicle carries its own ground truth. Every sortie that computes an index also drops the probe, so the calibration is generated in the same place, at the same time, under the same illumination, by the same machine. The point measurement extrapolates across the image; the image tells the point measurement where to look next.
the_architectural_insight.why_this_is_the_thesis: This is the defensible scientific contribution and it should be the centre of both the pitch and the dissertation for the Phase 3 offering. Not 'a drone with sensors' — a self-calibrating spatial sensing system where the two halves make each other work. State it in those terms.
the_architectural_insight.honest_caveat: Unproven. The calibration workflow must survive two different days and two different illumination conditions before this paragraph is anything more than a good idea. That is the Phase 3 hardware's own exit gate, distinct from the Phase 1/2 software gates.

what_the_system_is_not.purpose: Volunteering boundaries is the cheapest credibility available. Every item here is something a reader might otherwise assume about the Phase 3 hardware specifically.
items: Not a fish kill predictor. Not yet. The risk model is unvalidated and will be for at least a full season. Until then the claim is earlier detection, not prediction.
Not a toxin detector. It measures pigments correlated with taxa that sometimes produce toxins. A high chlorophyll reading is a reason to sample, not a diagnosis — and people eat the shellfish.
Not a disease diagnostic. It detects mortality and stress windows. A vet diagnoses.
Not a biomass estimator. No underwater vision. This is a real gap and it will eventually mean integrating with sonar vendors rather than competing with them.
Not autonomous in the decisions that matter. It does not medicate a pond on its own authority. Consequential actions stay with the person who owns the consequence.
Not a replacement for the farmer's experience. It extends that experience into the depths and hours where it has no access.
Not weatherproof. It cannot fly in the storms that trigger overturn events — which is a genuine limitation of the catastrophe-prevention story and the strongest argument for a fixed-buoy hybrid.
Not a surveillance platform. The IUU-enforcement application is deliberately not pursued; it would make adversaries of the communities we need as customers.

system_components.role: Shelter, charge, rinse, decontaminate, calibrate, backhaul. Converts a tool someone operates into infrastructure that runs.
Ingestion, fusion, AI/ML, storage, API — the same backend that serves Phases 1 and 2, extended here to hardware telemetry
The digital twin the farmer actually looks at

why_the_dock_matters_as_much_as_the_dron.note: Under-appreciated in most drone pitches, including, so far, ours.
reasoning: Autonomy is what makes the pre-dawn sortie possible, and the pre-dawn sortie is where the entire diel-bias argument lives. A drone that needs a human to launch it will never fly at 04:30, which means it will only ever measure the safest hour of the day — exactly the failure it was built to fix.
The dock is what lets the probe be dry between sorties, which is why we foul less than a moored sonde.
The dock holds the calibrated reflectance panel, without which the multispectral time series is not comparable across days.
The dock is the decontamination station, which is what makes multi-site operation biosecure rather than a pathogen distribution service.
The dock is the unit of network economics. Coverage is measured in docks, not drones.
why_the_dock_matters_as_much_as_the_dron.conclusion: Four of the system's five distinguishing claims depend on the dock, not the aircraft. The dock should be treated as a co-equal product, not an accessory.

operating_concept_summary.sketch: Dock on shore, solar or grid powered, connected. Scheduled sorties several times daily including pre-dawn. Each sortie: launch, transit, station-keep, profile, image, optionally dispense, next station, return, dock, rinse, charge, upload. Human oversight from the dashboard; human decision on every consequential action; certificated remote pilot in the loop until BVLOS authorisation permits otherwise.

maturity_statement.what_that_means: The physics is established in the literature. The integration into this vehicle is not demonstrated in hardware. Nothing here has flown.
maturity_statement.why_it_is_stated_prominently: Because the render is persuasive and the specification is detailed, and together they can create an impression of maturity that does not exist. Anyone reading this repository should leave knowing exactly what stage this specific Phase 3 layer is at — distinct from, and less mature than, the Phase 1 app concept.
